title: Foonar
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Species |image=[[File:Foonar1.png]] |name=Foonar |designation=Non-sentient |class= |subspecies= |races= |height= Over 0.8 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]The adult foonars are shown to be taller than [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]], which [[Databank]] gives his height at 0.8 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]. |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor= |haircolor=Grey |feathers= |eyecolor= |distinctions=*Horns *Scaled back |lifespan= |origin=[[Endor/Legends|Endor]] |habitat=[[Grass/Legends|Grassland]]s |diet= |language= }} '''Foonars''' were horned, scaly [[pachydermoid]]s found in the grasslands on the forest moon of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. == Biology and appearance == {{Quote|We're lucky Dooni has a strong back!''"
"''Yeah! Strong and scaly!|[[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa]] and [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket]] commenting while riding [[Dooni]].|}} Foonars were large quadrupedal [[pachydermoid]]s, covered with fur. The had a couple of horns and a series of scales that descended from their backs to their tails. Some members had colorful fur at the top of their heads.[[Ewoks (1985) 5|''Ewoks'' (1985) 5]] == History == {{Quote|I wish these beasts were more cooperative!|One of the harvesters while forcing the female foonar.|}} The [[Bright Tree Village tribe]] knew about a herd of foonars that lived in the [[Grass/Legends|grassland]], led by a [[Dooni's mother|huge female]]. Considered gentle beasts, at some point one young calf was named as [[Dooni]] by the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]], who knew that his mother would never go off and leave him alone. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]{{InsiderCite|23|Star Wars Publications Timeline|''Star Wars'' Publications Timeline}} dates the events of the [[Star Wars: Ewoks (1985)|''Ewoks'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, which include the events of ''Ewoks'' 5, to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], which corresponds to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. such herd of foonars was captured and forced in submission by a [[Rok's species|group of alien woodsmen]], led by an individual named [[Rok]]. He and his men wanted to used the foonars to push their [[Harvester/Legends|harvester]] to cut down the [[Tree/Legends|trees]], which included those holding the [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]]. [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]] and [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]] managed to active the harvester to take by surprise the invaders and, with the help of the female foonar, pushed the machines to threaten their [[Factory ship|ship]]. The harvesters fled the planet and the foonars ended their menace by pushing the harvester off a cliff, destroying it. == Behind the scenes == [[File:Eodon-HS.png|thumb|An [[Eodon]], another species of [[pachydermoid]]s found on [[Endor/Legends|Endor]].]] The Foonar species made its first appearance in the [[Ewoks (1985) 5|fifth issue]] of the [[1985]] [[Star Wars: Ewoks (1985)|''Star Wars: Ewoks'']] comic series, published in [[October 1]], [[1985]].''[[Marvel Age]]'' 32 In the comic issue it is stated that the foonars lived in the [[Grass/Legends|grassland]] of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. Such grassland could be referencing to the [[Endorian Grasslands]], first featured in the [[Star Wars: Ewoks|''Star Wars: Ewoks'']] episode "[[The Land of the Gupins]]"{{Ewoks|The Land of the Gupins}} and released the same month of the comic. However the comic was released 25 days before the airing of the TV episode in [[October 26]].{{ThisWeek|dTR7q_0_YOQ|The Mandalorian Virtual Red Carpet Premiere, What Scares the Jedi, and More!|sw_url=video/the-mandalorian-virtual-red-carpet-premiere-what-scares-the-jedi-and-more}} Another species of [[pachydermoid]]s, in the form of [[Eodon]], was shown to exist on Endor according to the episode "[[Hard Sell]]".{{Ewoks|Hard Sell}} Both species featured what appeared to be horns and a scaly back. No sources has ever acknowledged if the two species are in some way related. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Foonars}} *[[Ewoks (1985) 5|''Ewoks'' (1985) 5]] {{1st}} == Notes and references == {{Reflist}}{{Interlang |de=Foonar |es=Foonar }} [[Category:Creatures of Endor]] [[Category:Creatures of unspecified diet]] [[Category:Grassland and plains creatures]] [[Category:Species of undefined sentience]] [[Category:Vertebrates]]
